title: Stop Using the Sharpe Ratio Until You Watch This
description:

*📈 Interactive Brokers for Algorithmic Trading*

*🪐 Jupyter Notebook*
https://github.com/romanmichaelpaolucci/Quant-Guild-Library/blob/main/2026%20Video%20Lectures/101.%20Stop%20Using%20the%20Sharpe%20Ratio%20Until%20You%20Watch%20This/qas3m.ipynb

**📑 TL;DW Executive Summary**
* Realized performance metrics like the Sharpe Ratio are merely a "compression" of a single observed sample path. Infinite potential paths can produce the exact same realized statistics, but we only get to walk one.
* Variance is not just a risk metric; it is a mathematical "drag" on compounded wealth. Even with identical arithmetic returns, higher volatility erodes long-term geometric growth:
* The Sharpe Ratio penalizes "good" volatility (upside deviations) from the mean. If your strategy's variance is driven by large positive jumps, the Sortino Ratio is a superior diagnostic tool as it only penalizes downside semi-variance
* Traditional finance often assumes stationary distributions. In reality, markets undergo Regime Changes and Structural Breaks, which can turn a "perfect" backtest into a "crash" the moment it goes live.
* Chasing a high Sharpe Ratio in a backtest often results in overfitting to historical noise rather than capturing a repeatable signal. A robust, lower-Sharpe model often outperforms a "perfect" overfit model out-of-sample.
* Live strategy performance—actual decisions made in the face of true uncertainty, is worth 1,000 backtests. Quantitative finance is about robust decision-making and experience, not just optimizing historical ratios.

I hope you enjoyed, and I hope you learned something!

- Roman
___________________________________________

15:52 - How to Think About Trading and Quant Trading
